summary: Recent updates to the media control features in Firefox have focused on improving how audio is managed during browsing. A new mute control has been added to the parent-process MediaController, allowing for more efficient audio management across different tabs. Additionally, the browser now clears the audibility of discarded browsing contexts, which means that when you close or navigate away from a page, its audio settings will no longer interfere with any active media playback. These changes enhance user experience by ensuring that only relevant audio sources are heard and give users better control over their listening environment, making it easier to enjoy media without interruptions.
